headline: US-China one-year tariff truce agreed at Busan summit — reciprocal duties suspended until Nov 2026, chip tariffs delayed to 2027
summary: Trump and Xi agreed to a one-year tariff truce at a meeting in Busan, South Korea. The US will reduce fentanyl duties from 20% to 10%, lowering the overall tariff rate on Chinese imports from 41% to 31%. Reciprocal customs duties of 24% suspended until November 2026. New semiconductor tariffs delayed to at least mid-2027. China agreed to suspend rare earth export restrictions for one year.

headline: Oklo advances 12% after NRC approves Aurora reactor Principal Design Criteria — commercial ops targeted late 2027
summary: The US Nuclear Regulatory Commission approved the Principal Design Criteria for Oklo's Aurora microreactor. Oklo now has a 14 GW customer pipeline anchored by a 12 GW Switch deal and 500 MW Equinix agreement. Commercial operations targeted between late 2027 and early 2028.

headline: NANO Nuclear signs MOU with Supermicro to deploy microreactors for on-site datacenter power
summary: NANO Nuclear Energy and Super Micro Computer signed a Memorandum of Understanding to explore deploying NANO Nuclear microreactors for dedicated, on-site nuclear power at datacenters. The partnership aims to provide carbon-free baseload power directly at datacenter facilities.

headline: Eaton posts record Q1 revenue of $7.5B — datacenter orders surge 240% YoY, 228 GW backlog
summary: Eaton reported record Q1 2026 revenue of $7.5B with adjusted EPS of $2.81 (+17% YoY). Datacenter orders soared 240% driven by AI capacity buildout. The company estimates 32 GW of US datacenter capacity under construction, ~70% tied to AI. Eaton raised organic growth outlook by 200bps to 9-11% range.

headline: GE Vernova Q1 orders surge 71% to $18.3B — datacenter Electrification orders of $2.4B exceed all of 2025
summary: GE Vernova posted Q1 orders of $18.3B (+71% YoY) with revenue up 7% and adjusted EBITDA margin expanding 390bps. The Electrification segment booked $2.4B in datacenter equipment orders in Q1 alone, exceeding the full-year 2025 total. Management raised full-year revenue, EBITDA margin, and FCF guidance.

headline: Equinix posts record Q1 — revenue $2.44B (+10% YoY), record 51% EBITDA margin, raises full-year guidance
summary: Equinix reported record Q1 2026 revenue of $2.44B with a record 51% adjusted EBITDA margin. Net income rose 21% YoY to $415M. Management raised FY2026 guidance to $10.1-10.2B revenue and projected $4.1B capex to fund 46 active builds across 32 markets. 8 of top 10 AI model providers are expanding with Equinix.

headline: Hyperscaler 2026 capex estimates reach $660-690B — Alphabet raises to $180-190B, power constraints become primary bottleneck
summary: Combined hyperscaler capex for 2026 now estimated at $660-690B, with approximately 75% directed at AI infrastructure (~$450B in AI-specific spend). Alphabet raised guidance to $180-190B, while Amazon targets $200B. Power, cooling, and permitting — not silicon — identified as primary infrastructure constraints.

headline: Google Cloud revenue surges 63% to $20B in Q1 2026, outpacing Azure (40%) and AWS (28%) — AI becomes primary growth driver
summary: Google Cloud posted 63% YoY revenue growth to $20B in Q1, winning 14% cloud market share (up 2pp YoY). Azure grew 40% while AWS reached $37.6B (+28%). Google noted enterprise AI solutions became their primary cloud growth driver for the first time in Q1 2026. Combined hyperscaler cloud revenue hit $112B for the quarter.

headline: TSMC considers building angstrom-scale fab at Taoyuan Longtan Science Park — sub-2nm 1.6nm process targeted for H2 2026
summary: TSMC is planning an angstrom-scale semiconductor fab at Longtan Science Park in Taoyuan. The fab would produce chips using the 1.6nm process node, with production potentially beginning H2 2026. TSMC also confirmed it will defer deployment of ASML high-NA EUV lithography machines through 2029 to manage costs.

headline: Global AI investment activity surpasses $650B annually as enterprise adoption rate jumps from 22% to 40% in one year
summary: Enterprise AI adoption accelerated from 22% in 2025 to an expected 40% by end of 2026, driving annual AI investment past $650B. The investment spans model training infrastructure, inference compute, and enterprise deployment. Depreciation cycles of 5-6 years on AI infrastructure investments beginning to weigh on hyperscaler profit margins.

headline: NuScale Power Q1 revenue plunges 96% to $0.57M, missing consensus of $5.57M — stock down 27% YTD
summary: NuScale Power reported Q1 revenue of just $0.57M, a 96% decline from $13.38M in Q1 2025, well below the $5.57M consensus estimate. The SMR developer expects commercial reactor operations by 2030, significantly behind Oklo's 2027-2028 timeline. Stock is down nearly 27% year-to-date.

headline: Digital Realty raises 2026 capex guide by $250M to $3.5-4.0B — construction pipeline up 60% to 1.2 GW, 61% pre-leased
summary: Digital Realty increased its 2026 capex guidance by $250M to $3.5-4.0B range. The under-construction pipeline surged 60% sequentially to 1.2 GW at 11.4% expected yield, with 61% already pre-leased. The expansion reflects continued strong demand for hyperscale and AI datacenter capacity.
